question_text: Given Φ(1.645) = 0.95 and Φ(2.33) = 0.99. For a random sample X1, . . . , Xn ∼N(µ, 22) with µ unknown, test H0 : µ = 10 vs H1 : µ = 12 using a rule that rejects H0 when X is large. Find the smallest n (in integer) so that Type I error is 0.05

options.A: nd Type II error is at most 0.01.

solution:
Under H0, X ∼N(10, 4/n). The level-0.05 critical value c satisfies
PH0(X > c) = 0.05
X −10
2/√n > 1.645
c = 10 + 2 · 1.645
Power at µ = 12 is
P12(X > c) = P
X −12
2/√n > c −12
2/√n
= P�Z > −√n + 1.645
Type II error ≤0.01 means P12(X > c) ≥0.99, i.e.
Φ(√n −1.645) ≥0.99 =⇒√n −1.645 ≥2.33 =⇒√n ≥3.975.
Hence n ≥15.80, so the smallest integer is 16 .
Quick Tip
For known variance σ2 in one–sided tests, solve the size constraint for the critical value,
then impose the desired power under the alternative using the z–transform.
